subject: Volatility profiles
truth: Stop using profiles; use symbol tables generated from the memory image itself.
quote: Volatility 3 no longer uses profiles, it comes with an extensive library of symbol tables, and can generate new symbol tables for most windows memory images, based on the memory image itself.

subject: Object model — proxy objects replaced by direct Python inheritance
old: Volatility 2 proxy objects that emulated host methods but were a different type (could cause ordering-dependent failures like x + y vs y + x)
truth: Objects now inherit directly from their Python counterparts (e.g. an integer object is a real Python int) and can be used wherever the native type is expected.
quote: objects now inherit directly from their Python counterparts, meaning an integer object is actually a Python integer (and has all the associated methods, and can be used wherever a normal int could). In Volatility 2, a complex proxy object was constructed which tried to emulate all the methods of the host object, but ultimately it was a different type and could not be used in the same places (critically, it could make the ordering of operations important, since x + y might not work, but y + x might work fine).

from_fact: volatility 2 proxy objects that emulated host methods but were a different type (could cause ordering-dependent failures like x + y vs y + x)

subject: Data re-reading behaviour — live memory access
old: Volatility 2 re-read data from the underlying layer each time (allowing live memory changes to be reflected during plugin execution)
truth: Data is read once at object construction time and remains static even if the underlying layer changes.
quote: in Volatility 3 the data is now read once at the time of object construction, and will remain static, even if the underlying layer changes. This was because live memory analysis was barely ever used, and this feature could cause a particular value to be re-read many times over for no benefit

from_fact: volatility 2 re-read data from the underlying layer each time (allowing live memory changes to be reflected during plugin execution)

subject: Object metadata location — vol() dictionary
old: Volatility 2 stored metadata (layer, offset) as attributes on the object itself, which could conflict with arbitrary member names
truth: All metadata (layer, offset, etc.) has been moved to a read-only vol() dictionary, freeing member names for arbitrary use.
quote: all the metadata about the object (such as its layer or offset) have been moved to a read-only vol() dictionary.

from_fact: volatility 2 stored metadata (layer, offset) as attributes on the object itself, which could conflict with arbitrary member names

subject: Template vs Object distinction — size query
old: In Volatility 2, size could only be determined from a constructed object (requiring instantiation on an empty buffer)
truth: Templates now contain size information directly; you can query size without constructing the object.
quote: In Volatility 3, templates contain information such as their size, which can be queried directly without constructing the object.

from_fact: in volatility 2, size could only be determined from a constructed object (requiring instantiation on an empty buffer)

subject: Address spaces
old: address spaces (strictly stacked, one atop another)
truth: Replace 'address spaces' with 'translation layers' — layers can now have multiple dependencies instead of a strict stack.
quote: Address spaces in Volatility 2, are now more accurately referred to as Translation Layers, since each one typically sits atop another and can translate addresses between the higher logical layer and the lower physical layer. Address spaces in Volatility 2 were strictly limited to a stack, one on top of one other. In Volatility 3, layers can have multiple 'dependencies' (lower layers), which allows for the integration of features such as swap space.

from_fact: address spaces (strictly stacked, one atop another)

subject: Automagic
old: automagic was automatic and not user-configurable
new: automagic processes are codified, can be enabled/disabled, and are selected per-plugin (Linux vs Windows)
truth: Automagic is now per-plugin (Linux vs Windows) and can be enabled/disabled, rather than always running automatically.
quote: We've now codified that more, so that the automagic processes are clearly defined and can be enabled or disabled as necessary for any particular run.

from_fact: automagic was automatic and not user-configurable
automagic processes are codified, can be enabled/disabled, and are selected per-plugin (linux vs windows)

subject: Plugin output
old: plugins could produce output in various formats
new: all plugins must produce output in a TreeGrid object
truth: All plugins must now produce output as a TreeGrid object — other output formats are no longer accepted.
quote: We now require that all plugins produce output in a 'TreeGrid' object, which ensure that the library can be used regardless of which interface is driving it.

from_fact: plugins could produce output in various formats
all plugins must produce output in a treegrid object

subject: Volatility 2 plugins
truth: Volatility 2 plugins will not run on Volatility 3 and must be rewritten.
quote: Volatility plugins that were developed for Volatility 2 will not run on Volatility 3, and so it is necessary to update such plugins.

subject: plugin entry point
truth: Stop implementing calculate(); implement run() instead.

subject: table output functions
truth: Stop using table_header() and table_row(); build a TreeGrid() with typed columns instead.
quote: Volatility plugin has functions to display results. These are table_header() and table_row() in Volatility 2, replaced by TreeGrid() in Volatility 3.

subject: plugin base class
truth: Stop inheriting from Volatility 2 base classes; inherit from interfaces.plugins.PluginInterface instead.
quote: From Volatility 3, a new class interfaces.plugins.PluginInterface has been defined with the aim of integrating the plugin interface. Now developers inherit this class to develop their plugins.

subject: short_option
truth: short_option is no longer available in Volatility 3; use the requirements function instead.
quote: In Volatility 3, short_option is no longer available.

subject: option inheritance
old: options inherited automatically from base class (e.g. pid)
new: options must be explicitly declared in requirements
truth: Options like pid are no longer inherited automatically; they must be explicitly declared in the requirements function.
quote: options need to be set in Volatility 3. For example, pid, an option to specify process to analyse which works on many plugins, is not inherited, and rather needs to be written in the requirements.

from_fact: options inherited automatically from base class (e.g. pid)
options must be explicitly declared in requirements

subject: profile option for OS info
truth: The profile option is no longer used; set OS symbols via requirements (e.g. nt_symbols for Windows) instead.
quote: In Volatility 2, the OS information of the memory image to analyse has to be set with the profile option. This process is no longer necessary in Volatility 3, but instead it has to be set with the requirements function. (If it is Windows OS, use nt_symbols.)

subject: --profile option
truth: Stop using --profile; Volatility 3 auto-detects OS and configures symbols via get_requirements().
quote: The `--profile` option is removed; Volatility 3 auto-detects the OS and configures symbols via the `get_requirements()` function (e.g. `nt_symbols` for Windows).

subject: addrspace access
new: Memory Layer declared in get_requirements() and accessed through context.layers
truth: Stop using direct addrspace access; use a Memory Layer declared in get_requirements() and accessed via context.layers.
quote: Direct `addrspace` access is replaced by a Memory Layer declared in `get_requirements()` and accessed through `context.layers`.

from_fact: memory layer declared in get_requirements() and accessed through context.layers

subject: plugin option definition
old: Plugin options defined by class inheritance/argparse config
new: Plugin options declared in get_requirements() as requirements.* objects
truth: Stop defining plugin options via class inheritance or argparse; declare them in get_requirements() using requirements.* objects.
quote: Plugin options are no longer defined by class inheritance/argparse config; they are declared in `get_requirements()` as `requirements.*` objects.

from_fact: plugin options defined by class inheritance/argparse config
plugin options declared in get_requirements() as requirements.* objects
